title: Cisco ISE Patch and Upgrade Health — Pre/Post-Upgrade Validation
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative and Operational Audit* + *Internal Operations Diagnostics*. Codes — `51020` (patch installed), `51021` (patch rolled back), `51022` (upgrade to new version started), `51023` (upgrade completed), `51024` (upgrade failed), `51025` (bulk admin operation start). Health metrics during/after — auth-success rate (5400 vs 5440-5447 ratio), MnT lag, replication state. ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/upgrade/status` polled every 15 min during upgrade window.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (51020, 51021, 51022, 51023, 51024))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:upgrade_status")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event by node, MessageCode
| eval status = case(MessageCode=="51023", "upgrade_success", MessageCode=="51024", "upgrade_failed", MessageCode=="51020", "patch_success", MessageCode=="51021", "patch_rollback", MessageCode=="51022", "upgrade_started")
| where status IN ("upgrade_failed", "patch_rollback")
description: Detects ISE patch rollbacks, failed upgrades, and degraded post-upgrade health (auth-success rate drop or replication lag spike) within 24h after an upgrade event.
value: ISE upgrades are months-long projects across many nodes; a single failed step can leave the deployment in a degraded state. Detecting the failure within minutes shortens the recovery window. The post-upgrade health-check arm of this UC catches subtle regressions — a new ISE release breaking an obscure auth flow that only shows up in production.
implementation: Confirm logging categories above are at INFO. Deploy the upgrade-status poller (only active during upgrade windows). Schedule the search every 5 min during upgrade windows; daily otherwise.
visualization: (1) Single value: pending/in-flight upgrades. (2) Pre/post comparison panel: auth-success rate trend over 7 days bracketing the upgrade. (3) Top-N: nodes with longest upgrade duration.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Patch rollback during testing** — Lab patch rollback fires; tag and exclude. (b) **In-progress upgrade** — In a rolling upgrade, individual nodes report 'failed' transiently while waiting for the cluster to converge; only alert on terminal-state failures (51024).
mitreAttack: N/A (platform lifecycle — ISE upgrade rehearsal monitoring, no adversary technique)

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 6.3.3 patch within 1 month requirement — patch-success monitoring evidences the controls.
Art.21(2)(e) vulnerability handling — patch lifecycle audit.
A.8.8 (Management of technical vulnerabilities) — patch lifecycle.
ITGC change management — patches are changes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab ISE, install a patch that intentionally rolls back. ISE emits 51021. UC-17.1.49 fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Successful patch emits 51020 only. UC-17.1.49 does not fire.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Logging categories at INFO.
• Upgrade-status poller for windows.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Patch & Upgrade Lifecycle":
• Patch/upgrade history table.
• Pre/post auth-success comparison.
• Top-N upgrade-duration outliers.

Incident response: high-urgency PagerDuty on 51024 / 51021.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Upgrade stuck** — `application configure ise > 13` and inspect upgrade logs.
• **Patch rollback recurring** — Patch incompatible with existing customisation; engage TAC.
• **Post-upgrade auth drop** — Specific identity-store integration broken by the upgrade; review release notes.
grandmaExplanation: When ISE installs a software update or patch, we watch closely to make sure it succeeds and that user logins keep working afterwards. If the update fails or breaks something, we ring the bell immediately.
